claims.evidence_text: Wheat seeds were sown on 10th May 2018 and 23rd April 2019. Seed rates were 550 seeds m−2 as common in the region.
claims.claim_summary: Spring wheat sown at 550 seeds/m2, common for Central Sweden.

claims.evidence_text: At sowing, 140 kg ha−1 of N, 24 kg ha−1 of P and 46 kg ha−1 of K were applied.
claims.claim_summary: 140 kg/ha N applied at sowing to spring wheat.

claims.evidence_text: At sowing, 140 kg ha−1 of N, 24 kg ha−1 of P and 46 kg ha−1 of K were applied.
claims.claim_summary: 24 kg/ha P applied at sowing to spring wheat.

claims.evidence_text: At sowing, 140 kg ha−1 of N, 24 kg ha−1 of P and 46 kg ha−1 of K were applied.
claims.claim_summary: 46 kg/ha K applied at sowing to spring wheat.

claims.evidence_text: the pH (H2O) was 5.8 and the particle density was 2.61 Mg m−3
claims.claim_summary: Silt loam Cambisol soil for spring wheat had pH(H2O) of 5.8 in upper 0.3 m.

claims.evidence_text: The soil is classiﬁed as Cambisol (WRB, 2014) with a silt loam texture (16% clay, 70% silt, 14% sand) and an organic matter of 4% in the uppermost 0.3 m.
claims.claim_summary: Spring wheat grown on silt loam Cambisol (16% clay, 70% silt, 14% sand), 4% organic matter.

claims.evidence_text: The critical value of penetration resistance for being a major limitation to root growth may vary... but is assumed to be between 2 and 3.5 MPa
claims.claim_summary: Soil penetration resistance of 2-3.5 MPa is critical limitation for wheat root growth; high penetration resistance (not bulk density) reduces wheat growth and yield.

claims.evidence_text: Air-ﬁlled porosity... a threshold value of <10% is regarded as critical for plant growth (Lipiec and Hatano, 2003).
claims.claim_summary: Soil air-filled porosity below 10% is critical for wheat growth (aeration/drainage threshold).

claims.evidence_text: harvested with a combine harvester on 17th August 2018 (i.e., 99 days after sowing) and 23rd August 2019 (i.e., 122 days after sowing)
claims.claim_summary: Spring wheat reached maturity/harvest at 99-122 days after sowing across two seasons in Central Sweden.
